text.title: Acceptance Criteria Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real applications of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: AC body uses vague verbs ('should be user-friendly', 'performant').
antipattern.root-cause: Author writes UX-style descriptions instead of behaviour.
antipattern.fix: Force AC body to assert a measurable observable; reject vague verbs via authoring prompt.

antipattern.symptom: Single story has 12+ AC; story is implicitly an epic.
antipattern.fix: Enforce maxItems=7 per story; split when exceeded.

antipattern.symptom: AC exists but no link to spec.md or test file.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats AC as documentation only.
antipattern.fix: Require trace.spec_ref + trace.test_ref per AC bundle; reject orphans.
